introSection:
Thunder Striker, originally known as Intimidator, is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it was the park's thirteenth roller coaster when it opened on March 27, 2010. It was the tallest roller coaster at the park until the opening of Fury 325 in 2015.

The attraction was built on the former site of the Wild Thornberry's River Adventure log flume, which was closed in 2008.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
At the end of the 2008 season, Carowinds took down Wild Thornberry's River Adventure. At that point, it was decided that the ride had reached the end of its service life and it was time for something new.

Carowinds announced the addition of Intimidator on August 26, 2009. At a cost of $23 million, it was the most costly project in the park's history. Earlier that month, track pieces had arrived at the park.

On October 13, 2009, the lift hill was topped off. The final piece of track was installed in December 2009. Testing began on February 4, 2010.

Intimidator opened on March 27, 2010.

Seat belts were added to supplement the lap-bar restraints in 2014.

The coaster was given a new paint job in 2020.

On February 28, 2024, it was announced that Intimidator would be renamed Thunder Striker. This coincided with the announcement of a retheme of Intimidator 305 at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: As the train is dispatched, "Gentlemen, start your engines!" is played on the speakers. Riders begin to climb up the tall chain lift hill before dropping. The train then heads through a tall banked hill. This is followed by a tall hill and a  tall horseshoe. Riders go pass a hill, which is tall. A tall hill leads into the block brake. After this, the train goes through a tall hill and a helix. The last two hills lead into the final brake run. Riders turn left and return to the station.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content: When known as Intimidator, the ride was named after NASCAR driver Dale Earnhardt and the trains were designed to look like his 1998 No.3 car. Another roller coaster with the same theme, Intimidator 305, opened in the same year at Kings Dominion.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains employ staggered seating and riders are held in by lap-bars and seat belts.

fullText:
Thunder Striker, originally known as Intimidator, is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it was the park's thirteenth roller coaster when it opened on March 27, 2010. It was the tallest roller coaster at the park until the opening of Fury 325 in 2015.

The attraction was built on the former site of the Wild Thornberry's River Adventure log flume, which was closed in 2008.

At the end of the 2008 season, Carowinds took down Wild Thornberry's River Adventure. At that point, it was decided that the ride had reached the end of its service life and it was time for something new.

Carowinds announced the addition of Intimidator on August 26, 2009. At a cost of $23 million, it was the most costly project in the park's history. Earlier that month, track pieces had arrived at the park.

On October 13, 2009, the lift hill was topped off. The final piece of track was installed in December 2009. Testing began on February 4, 2010.

Intimidator opened on March 27, 2010.

Seat belts were added to supplement the lap-bar restraints in 2014.

The coaster was given a new paint job in 2020.

On February 28, 2024, it was announced that Intimidator would be renamed Thunder Striker. This coincided with the announcement of a retheme of Intimidator 305 at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA.



As the train is dispatched, "Gentlemen, start your engines!" is played on the speakers. Riders begin to climb up the tall chain lift hill before dropping. The train then heads through a tall banked hill. This is followed by a tall hill and a  tall horseshoe. Riders go pass a hill, which is tall. A tall hill leads into the block brake. After this, the train goes through a tall hill and a helix. The last two hills lead into the final brake run. Riders turn left and return to the station.

When known as Intimidator, the ride was named after NASCAR driver Dale Earnhardt and the trains were designed to look like his 1998 No.3 car. Another roller coaster with the same theme, Intimidator 305, opened in the same year at Kings Dominion.

The trains employ staggered seating and riders are held in by lap-bars and seat belts.
